DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT GRETCHEN KAH'S MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER TO PREVENT PLAINTIFF FROM ENTERING DEFENDANT'S HOME IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF'S ATTORNEY AND/OR HIS EXPERTS AND CONSULTANTS MAY ENTER DEFENDANT'S HOME TO INSPECT THE ITEMS LISTED IN THE SECOND AMENDED DEMAND FOR INSPECTION. THE INSPECTION MAY BE VIDEOTAPED OR PHOTOGRAPHED. PLAINTIFF SHALL HAVE TEN DAYS TO REVIEW THE VIDEO AND/OR PHOTOS. FOLLOWING PLAINTIFF'S REVIEW, HE MAY DESIGNATE ANY ITEMS WHICH HE BELIEVES REQUIRE FURTHER PERSONAL INSPECTION BY HIM TO BE DELIVERED (COSTS TO BE SPLIT BY THE PARTIES-) TO HIS ATTORNEY'S OFFICE FOR FURTHER INSPECTION. SUCH ITEMS SHALL BE DELIVERED WITHIN TEN DAYS OF PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL'S WRITTEN NOTICE TO DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL. PLAINTIFF SHALL THEN HAVE TWENTY DAYS TO PERSONALLY INSPECT AND RETURN THE REQUESTED ITEMS. ALTERNATIVELY, AT DEFENDANT'S OPTION, PLAINTIFF MAY ENTER DEFENDANT'S HOUSE FOR INSPECTION ON TEN DAYS NOTICE ON THE CONDITION THAT PLAINTIFF BE ACCOMPANIED BY HIS ATTORNEY AND THAT ANY COMMUNICATION MAY ONLY BE BETWEEN THE PARTIES' ATTORNEYS. NO SANCTIONS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO-TEM: TOM COHEN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED. =302/JPT

Notice And Motion For Protective Order To Prevent Plaintiff From Entering Defendant'S Home
Off Calendar Sep-09-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. GEORGE M. LEE OF SEILER EPSTEIN ET. AL., TEL. NO. 1-415-979-0500 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. ASIT PANWALA, TEL. NO. 1-415-766-3526 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT GRETCHEN KAH'S Motion For Protective Order To Prevent Plaintiff From Entering Defendant'S Home is granted in part and denied in part as follows: Plaintiff's attorney and/or his experts and consultants may enter defendant's home to inspect the items listed in the Second Amended Demand for Inspection. The inspection may be videotaped or photographed. Plaintiff shall have ten days to review the video and/or photos. Following Plaintiff's review, he may designate any items which he believes require further personal inspection by him to be delivered (COSTS TO BE SPLIT BY THE PARTIES-) to his attorney's office for further inspection. Such items shall be delivered within ten days of Plaintiff's counsel's written notice to Defendant's counsel. Plaintiff shall then have twenty days to personally inspect and return the requested items. Alternatively, at Defendant's option, Plaintiff may enter Defendant's house for inspection on ten days notice on the condition that Plaintiff be accompanied by his attorney and that any communication may only be between the parties' attorneys. No sanctions. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro-tem: Tom Cohen; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not Reported. =302/jpt
